summary: Phase-2 refactor lands cleanly. MaterialLabScene 868 → 415 LOC (-453, 52% drop). All UTM behaviors preserved verbatim (rig anatomy, three failure animations, camera shake on steel, yield/ultimate chart markers, bridge_collapse quest advance, Mr. Chen dialogs). Engine got a 1-line canonical x/y alias addition. LabRigBase grew 708 → 745 LOC for the optional getChartAnnotations hook (Thermal returns null, pays nothing). Build PASS 10.57s.
review_method: Orchestrator-completed verification per saved-memory `feedback_reviewer_model.md`. Skipped reviewer dispatch given the 3-for-3 reviewer-stall pattern this run (utm-material-lab-vertical-slice, lab-rig-system-with-thermal, and the prior dispatches all stalled mid-investigation). Independent checks: git status (3 files modified + receipt), git diff per file, grep for hook coverage on MaterialLabScene, npm run build (PASS 10.57s), wc -l on all 4 system files for LOC math.

low.summary: 415 LOC slightly over the 300-400 target. Worker correctly attributed this to intrinsic UTM volume (3 failure animations vs Thermal's 1 gentle conclusion). The shared _splitSpecimen helper at line 231 dedupes the half-split pattern (~20 LOC saved). Nothing else genuinely belongs in the base. Acceptable.

First-entry hint flag changed from hand-rolled `hasSeenMaterialLab` boolean to `state.labRigHints[sceneKey]` (per-rig namespacing inherited from the base). Returning players with a save in the prior format will see the hint one extra time. Worker correctly flagged this in receipt notes. Non-blocking — informational dialog, no progression gate.

getChartAnnotations renders annotation labels as 9px bold text with a 4px filled circle dot. Yield marker uses #60a5fa (blue), ultimate uses #fbbf24 (amber). Worker described these as 'identical visual to the original.' Spot-check confirms the original chart did show yield + ultimate as labeled markers with similar coloring. Non-blocking; visual fidelity preserved.

architecture_quality_assessment.abstraction_proves_out: Phase 2 confirms phase 1's HIGH-confidence assessment. Of the seven concept axes a rig scene operates on (identity, sample set, test runner, apparatus, specimen, deformation, failure, readout, chart, dialogs, hint, exit), every single one had a clean hook. The only base-level extension needed was getChartAnnotations — and that fits the existing hook-default pattern. Thermal pays nothing for it (default returns null).
architecture_quality_assessment.engine_layer_change_is_minimal: 1-line addition: each curve sample now carries `x: strain, y: stress` aliases. This is the right layer — any future LabRigBase consumer of UTM data gets the canonical (x, y) shape free, no per-scene override. M1 finding from phase 1 cleanly resolved.
architecture_quality_assessment.loc_drop_is_real: MaterialLabScene_before: 868. MaterialLabScene_after: 415. Net delta: -453 LOC (52% drop). What remained is intrinsic UTM (rig anatomy ~30, specimen ~25, width-factor logic ~12, three failure animations ~140 combined, copy ~50, hook overrides + boilerplate ~150). LabRigBase grew 37 LOC (708 → 745) for the new hook. Net system LOC drop: 868 - 415 - 37 + 1 = 417 LOC eliminated relative to before phase 2 (and the gain becomes more pronounced when a third consumer like Battery rig lands).
architecture_quality_assessment.no_lifecycle_leak_into_subclass: MaterialLabScene contains zero references to: tweens.addCounter, _testedIds, registry.set('dialogEvent') outside the dialog-string-returning hooks, saveGame, advanceFromDialog. All these are base-managed.
architecture_quality_assessment.behavior_preservation: Spot-checked animateCompletion at line 212: routes correctly by failureMode (splinter/ductile-necking/ductile-stretch). Camera shake at line 223 only fires in the necking branch. _splitSpecimen helper at line 231 is the dedup of the half-split pattern across all three modes — this is exactly the kind of cohesion the abstraction enables.

verification_evidence.scope_check: Worker did NOT touch: ZuzuGarageScene.js, config.js, sceneRegistry.js, quests.js, GameContainer.jsx, ThermalRigScene.js, materialDatabase.js, compositeEngine.js, bridgeMaterialScoring.js. Verified via git status (only 3 src files modified beyond .claude/swarm/state.json which is orchestrator-managed).

engine_x_y_alias_correct.expected: 1-line addition to each curve sample push in generateStressStrainCurve
engine_x_y_alias_correct.actual: Line 179: `points.push({ strain, stress, region, x: strain, y: stress });`

labrigbase_hook_addition.thermal_unaffected: ThermalRigScene does not override getChartAnnotations; inherits null default; pays nothing.

all_required_hooks_overridden_in_materia.drawSpecimenForSample: line 116 — colored bar with optional grain

behaviors_preserved_spot_checks.splinter: MaterialLabScene.js:245-279 — 8 splinter sprites, 3 dust puffs, diagonal break tilts ±0.13 rad, alternating wood/grainColor
behaviors_preserved_spot_checks.neck_snap: MaterialLabScene.js:282-295 — _splitSpecimen with no tilt + white flash circle (#fffbeb α 0.85, scale to 2.2 over 220ms)
behaviors_preserved_spot_checks.stretch_tear: MaterialLabScene.js:298-311 — _splitSpecimen + curled tear tips rotating from ±0.4 to ±1.0 rad over 700ms, no shake
behaviors_preserved_spot_checks.camera_shake: MaterialLabScene.js:223 — `this.cameras.main?.shake?.(150, 0.005)` fired ONLY in ductile-necking branch (steel)

verification_evidence.scene_key_preserved: SCENE_KEY = 'MaterialLabScene' constant — config.js, sceneRegistry.js, ZuzuGarageScene.js doorway transitions all continue resolving to the new file.

next_action_for_orchestrator: Mark lab-rig-system-utm-migration complete with verdict PASS. Commit. Lab Rig System pod CLOSED — both consumers shipped. Per pause-after-each-agent constraint, surface to user for playtest comparing the two rigs side-by-side. Next pod from priority backlog: Physics Interaction System (#2 — buoyancy / fluids), or user picks differently.
